title: BC Science Connections 10 — Full Course Knowledge Point Checklist
howToHtml:
How to Use This Checklist

- Read the textbook section.

- Check off each Knowledge Point after understanding it.

- Review formulas and common error areas before quizzes/tests.

- Use optional extensions and investigations for enrichment, projects, or lab review.
fullSummaryHtml:
Full Course Summary

- Total Units: 4

- Total Chapters: 16

- Total Core Sections: 55

- Total Core Knowledge Points: 364

- Optional Extension / Investigation Sections: 64

- Optional Extension / Investigation Knowledge Points: 208

Top 10 Core Formulas Across the Course

  1. Complementary DNA base pairing: \(A \leftrightarrow T\), \(G \leftrightarrow C\)
  2. DNA information flow: DNA \(\rightarrow\) RNA \(\rightarrow\) protein
  3. Human chromosome organization: 46 chromosomes \(= 23\) pairs \(= 22\) autosome pairs \(+ 1\) sex chromosome pair
  4. Mechanical kinetic energy: \(E_k = \frac{1}{2}mv^2\)
  5. Gravitational potential energy: \(E_g = mg\Delta h\)
  6. Specific heat capacity: \(Q = mc\Delta T\)
  7. Cellular respiration: \(C_6H_{12}O_6 + 6O_2 \rightarrow 6CO_2 + 6H_2O + \text{energy}\)
  8. Photosynthesis: \(6CO_2 + 6H_2O + \text{energy} \rightarrow C_6H_{12}O_6 + 6O_2\)
  9. Hubble’s law pattern: \(v = Hd\)
  10. Light-year distance: \(1\,ly \approx 9.46 \times 10^{12}\,km\)

Top 10 Common Error Areas Across the Course

  1. Confusing nucleotide, DNA, gene, allele, chromosome, chromatin, genome, and protein.
  2. Confusing genotype with phenotype.
  3. Treating Punnett square probabilities as guaranteed outcomes.
  4. Saying individual organisms evolve instead of populations changing over generations.
  5. Changing chemical subscripts instead of coefficients when balancing equations.
  6. Confusing ionic compounds, covalent compounds, molecules, and formula units.
  7. Confusing energy transfer with energy transformation.
  8. Treating “lost” energy as destroyed instead of transformed into less useful forms.
  9. Confusing radiation, conduction, convection, greenhouse gases, and greenhouse effect.
  10. Confusing constellations, asterisms, galaxies, star clusters, solar systems, nebulae, redshift, blueshift, dark matter, and dark energy.

Suggested Full-Course Learning Order

  1. DNA structure, DNA function, genes, alleles, chromosomes, proteins, and biodiversity.
  2. Mendelian inheritance, Punnett squares, codominance, incomplete dominance, and sex-linked inheritance.
  3. Mutation, genetic variation, natural selection, speciation, extinction, artificial selection, and biotechnology.
  4. Chemistry in daily life, chemical safety, WHMIS, SDS, and environmental chemical risk.
  5. Ionic and covalent bonding, chemical reactions, conservation of mass, and balanced chemical equations.
  6. Reaction energy, activation energy, exothermic/endothermic reactions, and reaction classification.
  7. Energy forms, energy transfer, energy transformation, conservation of energy, and energy equations.
  8. Chemical, nuclear, light, Earth-system, and human energy transformations.
  9. Universe, sky observation, celestial sphere, constellations, telescopes, galaxies, stars, and distance scales.
  10. Big bang theory, redshift, Hubble’s law, cosmic microwave background radiation, dark matter, and dark energy.

Suggested Full-Course Review Order

  1. Review key vocabulary chains:

    2. • DNA → gene → allele → chromosome → trait → protein

    • reactant → product → equation → coefficient → conservation of mass

    • system → surroundings → energy transfer → energy transformation

    • star → star cluster → galaxy → galaxy group → supercluster

  1. Review high-error comparisons:

    2. • genotype vs phenotype

    • codominance vs incomplete dominance

    • natural selection vs artificial selection

    • ionic vs covalent bonding

    • coefficient vs subscript

    • exothermic vs endothermic

    • energy transfer vs energy transformation

    • constellation vs asterism

    • redshift vs blueshift

    • dark matter vs dark energy

  1. Review formulas and symbolic patterns:

    2. • DNA base-pairing

    • Punnett-square ratios

    • reaction patterns

    • energy equations

    • photosynthesis and cellular respiration

    • astronomical distance units

    • Hubble’s law

  1. Review applications and risk-benefit reasoning:

    2. • biotechnology and ethics

    • chemical safety and pollution

    • energy technologies and environmental impacts

    • nuclear power and waste

    • light pollution and astronomy

    • terraforming and human responsibility
